metadata.title: Comprehensive Human Muscle Database
metadata.notes: Atrophy rates and strength loss percentages are approximate values based on exercise science literature. Individual variation exists based on age, genetics, nutrition, and baseline fitness.
metadata.sources_basis: Exercise physiology research, anatomical references, sports medicine literature

rapid.label: Rapid
rapid.description: Fast-twitch dominant muscles with minimal daily use

moderate.label: Moderate
moderate.description: Mixed fiber type muscles with some incidental daily use

slow.label: Slow
slow.description: Slow-twitch dominant or postural muscles with regular low-level activation

very_slow.label: Very Slow
very_slow.description: Muscles with constant involuntary or postural engagement

recovery_difficulty_scale.easy: Responds quickly to training, 2-4 weeks to regain baseline
recovery_difficulty_scale.moderate: Requires consistent effort, 4-8 weeks to regain baseline
recovery_difficulty_scale.hard: Slow to rebuild, 8-16 weeks, may need targeted isolation work
recovery_difficulty_scale.very_hard: Requires specialized rehabilitation, 16+ weeks, often needs professional guidance

week_1_2.details: Motor unit recruitment efficiency drops. Strength loss is primarily neural, not muscular. Glycogen stores decrease. No visible muscle loss yet.

week_2_4.details: Type II (fast-twitch) fibers begin to shrink. Glutes, spinal erectors, and deep stabilizers start weakening from prolonged sitting. Hip flexors begin to shorten. Upper trapezius may become overactive from desk posture.

week_4_8.details: Noticeable reduction in muscle tone in glutes, quads, and shoulders. Core stabilizers significantly weakened. Postural imbalances become established. Muscle protein synthesis rate drops substantially.

week_8_12.details: Substantial loss of muscle cross-sectional area (10-15% in major muscles). Tendons and connective tissue begin to weaken. Joint stability compromised. Metabolic rate decreases noticeably.

week_12_24.details: Muscle fiber type conversion begins (Type II to Type I). Significant postural dysfunction. Chronic pain patterns may develop. Bone density starts decreasing. Neuromuscular coordination substantially impaired.

week_24_plus.details: Muscles adapt to sedentary state. Fat infiltration of muscle tissue (myosteatosis). Severe postural dysfunction. High injury risk with sudden activity. Full recovery requires months of progressive training.

walking_30min.primary_benefit: Maintains baseline lower body and postural muscle tone

cycling_30min.primary_benefit: Strong quad and glute maintenance with low joint impact

running_30min.primary_benefit: Comprehensive lower body and core maintenance with cardiovascular benefit

swimming_30min.primary_benefit: Most comprehensive full-body muscle activation with zero impact

upper_crossed_syndrome.description: Common postural dysfunction from prolonged sitting at a desk

lower_crossed_syndrome.description: Pelvic and lower body dysfunction from prolonged sitting

common_pain_points.cause: Upper trapezius and levator scapulae overactivation from forward head posture
Weakened core and glutes combined with compressed lumbar discs from sitting
Overstretched and weakened rhomboids and middle trapezius from rounded shoulders
Chronic shortening of hip flexors from prolonged sitting
Repetitive strain from keyboard and mouse use
Gluteal amnesia from prolonged compression and disuse

muscle_imbalances.correction: Rows, face pulls, reverse flyes, chest stretching

muscle_imbalances.ratio_typical_desk_worker: Hip flexors significantly overactive relative to glutes
muscle_imbalances.consequence: Anterior pelvic tilt, lower back pain, poor hip extension
muscle_imbalances.correction: Glute bridges, hip thrusts, hip flexor stretching, glute activation drills

muscle_imbalances.ratio_typical_desk_worker: Upper traps 50-60% more active than lower traps
muscle_imbalances.consequence: Shoulder elevation, neck pain, scapular dyskinesis
muscle_imbalances.correction: Y-raises, wall slides, lower trap activation exercises

muscle_imbalances.ratio_typical_desk_worker: Erectors compensating for weak transversus abdominis and multifidus
muscle_imbalances.consequence: Lower back pain, spinal instability, disc issues
muscle_imbalances.correction: Dead bugs, bird dogs, planks, diaphragmatic breathing

muscle_imbalances.ratio_typical_desk_worker: Quads often 40-50% stronger than hamstrings (ideal is 60-70% ratio)
muscle_imbalances.consequence: Knee pain, ACL vulnerability, poor posterior chain function
muscle_imbalances.correction: Romanian deadlifts, hamstring curls, Nordic curls

muscle_groups.region: Head, face, jaw, and cervical spine

muscles.location: Forehead, from galea aponeurotica to skin of eyebrow
muscles.primary_function: Raises eyebrows and wrinkles forehead

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal impact - used in facial expressions throughout day

muscles.location: Back of skull, from superior nuchal line to galea aponeurotica
muscles.primary_function: Pulls scalp backward

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal impact

muscles.location: Side of skull in temporal fossa
muscles.primary_function: Closes jaw, assists in chewing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: May become overactive from stress clenching

muscles.location: Side of jaw from zygomatic arch to mandible
muscles.primary_function: Primary jaw closer, strongest bite force muscle

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Often becomes hypertonic from stress-related jaw clenching

muscles.location: Deep to mandible, from pterygoid plate to inner mandible
muscles.primary_function: Closes jaw, assists lateral jaw movement

activity_impact.desk_job_only: May become overactive with stress clenching

muscles.location: Deep to mandible, from pterygoid plate to TMJ disc and condyle
muscles.primary_function: Opens jaw, protrudes mandible, lateral jaw movement

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become dysfunctional with TMJ disorders from stress

muscles.location: Cheek, between maxilla and mandible
muscles.primary_function: Compresses cheek against teeth, aids chewing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintained through eating and speaking

muscles.location: Surrounding the eye socket
muscles.primary_function: Closes eyelids, squinting, blinking

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Slightly activated from wind exposure squinting
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Screen staring may reduce blink rate, causing strain not atrophy

muscles.location: Surrounding the mouth
muscles.primary_function: Closes and purses lips, speech articulation

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintained through speaking and eating

muscles.location: Side of neck, from sternum and clavicle to mastoid process
muscles.primary_function: Flexes and rotates the head, assists in breathing

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderately activated - holds head up while in cycling position
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Often becomes tight and overactive from forward head posture

muscles.location: Side of neck, from cervical transverse processes to first rib
muscles.primary_function: Lateral neck flexion, assists breathing by elevating first rib

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mild activation as accessory breathing muscle
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderate activation from breathing and neck position
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight from poor posture, contributing to thoracic outlet syndrome

muscles.location: Side of neck, from cervical transverse processes to first rib
muscles.primary_function: Lateral neck flexion, elevates first rib during breathing

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mild activation as accessory breathing muscle
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight and restricted

muscles.location: Side of neck, from cervical transverse processes to second rib
muscles.primary_function: Lateral neck flexion, elevates second rib during breathing

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mild activation as accessory breathing muscle
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight and restricted

muscles.location: Superficial sheet across front of neck from jaw to chest
muscles.primary_function: Depresses jaw, tenses skin of neck

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal impact - used in facial expressions

muscles.location: Under the chin, two bellies connecting mandible to hyoid to mastoid
muscles.primary_function: Opens jaw, elevates hyoid bone during swallowing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintained through eating and swallowing

muscles.location: Floor of mouth, from mandible to hyoid bone
muscles.primary_function: Elevates floor of mouth and hyoid, assists swallowing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintained through swallowing and speech

muscles.location: Deep anterior cervical spine, along vertebral bodies C1-T3
muscles.primary_function: Deep neck flexor, stabilizes cervical spine

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes WEAK and inhibited - key muscle in forward head posture correction

muscles.location: Deep anterior neck, from cervical transverse processes to occipital bone
muscles.primary_function: Flexes head and upper cervical spine

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes weak - contributes to forward head posture when inhibited

muscles.location: Posterior neck, from spinous processes to mastoid and occipital bone
muscles.primary_function: Extends and rotates the head

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight from sustained head extension to view screen

muscles.location: Posterior neck, from thoracic spinous processes to cervical transverse processes
muscles.primary_function: Extends and rotates cervical spine

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight and overloaded

muscles.location: Deep posterior neck, from thoracic/cervical transverse processes to occipital bone
muscles.primary_function: Extends head and cervical spine, maintains head posture

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderately activated from sustained neck extension
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Often overworked compensating for weak deep neck flexors

muscles.latin_name: Musculi suboccipitales (Rectus capitis posterior major/minor, Obliquus capitis superior/inferior)
muscles.location: Deep at base of skull between C1-C2 and occipital bone
muscles.primary_function: Fine control of head movements, proprioception of head position

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mildly activated for balance and gaze stabilization
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Become chronically tight and shortened - major cause of tension headaches

muscle_groups.region: Shoulder girdle, scapula, and glenohumeral joint

muscles.location: Front of shoulder, from lateral clavicle to deltoid tuberosity of humerus
muscles.primary_function: Flexes and internally rotates the arm

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Minimal - slight isometric from handlebar support
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly - rarely loaded overhead at desk

muscles.location: Top of shoulder, from acromion to deltoid tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Abducts the arm (lifts to the side)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly - arm abduction rarely needed at desk

muscles.location: Rear of shoulder, from scapular spine to deltoid tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Extends and externally rotates the arm

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies quickly - contributes to rounded shoulder posture when weak

muscles.location: Above scapular spine in supraspinous fossa
muscles.primary_function: Initiates arm abduction (first 15 degrees), stabilizes shoulder joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens moderately - most commonly injured rotator cuff muscle

muscles.location: Below scapular spine in infraspinous fossa
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates arm, stabilizes shoulder joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens significantly - internal rotation dominance from desk posture

muscles.location: Lateral border of scapula to greater tubercle of humerus
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates arm, stabilizes shoulder joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens - part of the rotator cuff imbalance

muscles.location: Inferior angle of scapula to intertubercular groove of humerus
muscles.primary_function: Adducts, extends, and internally rotates the arm

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies - no pulling movements at desk

muscles.location: Anterior surface of scapula (subscapular fossa) to lesser tubercle of humerus
muscles.primary_function: Internally rotates arm, stabilizes shoulder joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: May become tight from internal rotation desk posture

muscles.location: From cervical transverse processes (C1-C4) to superior angle of scapula
muscles.primary_function: Elevates scapula, assists in neck lateral flexion

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes CHRONICALLY TIGHT and overactive - major source of neck/shoulder pain

muscles.location: Lateral ribs (1-9) to medial border of scapula (anterior surface)
muscles.primary_function: Protracts scapula, rotates scapula upward, stabilizes scapula against ribcage

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderate activation from leaning on handlebars
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens significantly - contributes to scapular winging and poor shoulder mechanics

muscles.location: Occipital bone and cervical spinous processes to lateral clavicle
muscles.primary_function: Elevates and upwardly rotates scapula, extends neck

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes OVERACTIVE and hypertonic - main driver of neck tension

muscles.location: Upper thoracic spinous processes to acromion and scapular spine
muscles.primary_function: Retracts (squeezes together) scapulae

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and lengthens - key contributor to rounded shoulders

muscles.location: Lower thoracic spinous processes to medial scapular spine
muscles.primary_function: Depresses and upwardly rotates scapula

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens significantly - poor scapular control and shoulder impingement risk

muscles.location: Spinous processes T2-T5 to medial border of scapula
muscles.primary_function: Retracts and elevates scapula, rotates scapula downward

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and lengthens - contributes to inter-scapular pain

muscles.location: Spinous processes C7-T1 to medial border of scapula (above rhomboid major)
muscles.primary_function: Retracts and elevates scapula

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens alongside rhomboid major

muscles.location: Anterior upper arm, from scapula (two heads) to radial tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Flexes elbow, supinates forearm

activity_impact.walking_30min: Minimal - slight isometric from arm swing
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies rapidly - no significant loading at desk

muscles.location: Deep anterior upper arm, from mid-humerus to ulnar tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Primary elbow flexor (strongest elbow flexor)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintains some tone from carrying objects but overall atrophies

muscles.location: Lateral forearm, from lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus to styloid process of radius
muscles.primary_function: Flexes elbow (especially in neutral/hammer grip position)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Partially maintained from mouse/keyboard use

muscles.location: Posterior upper arm, three heads from scapula and humerus to olecranon of ulna
muscles.primary_function: Extends the elbow, long head assists shoulder extension

activity_impact.walking_30min: Minimal - slight activation in arm swing
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly - one of the fastest to lose tone

muscles.location: Small muscle on posterior elbow, from lateral epicondyle to olecranon
muscles.primary_function: Assists elbow extension, stabilizes elbow joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Mild activation from desk activities

muscles.location: Proximal forearm, from medial epicondyle of humerus to lateral radius
muscles.primary_function: Pronates forearm (turns palm down)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Partially maintained from keyboard use but can become tight/inflamed

muscles.location: Deep distal forearm, between distal ulna and radius
muscles.primary_function: Primary forearm pronator

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Activated during keyboard and mouse use

muscles.location: Deep proximal forearm, from lateral epicondyle and ulna to proximal radius
muscles.primary_function: Supinates forearm (turns palm up)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Underused compared to pronators - imbalance develops

muscles.location: Anterior forearm, from medial epicondyle to base of 2nd metacarpal
muscles.primary_function: Flexes and radially deviates the wrist

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Actively used in typing - can develop repetitive strain

muscles.location: Medial anterior forearm, from medial epicondyle and olecranon to pisiform bone
muscles.primary_function: Flexes and ulnarly deviates the wrist

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Actively used in typing - can develop strain

muscles.location: Superficial anterior forearm, from medial epicondyle to palmar aponeurosis
muscles.primary_function: Weak wrist flexor, tenses palmar fascia

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal impact - note: absent in about 14% of population

muscles.location: Intermediate anterior forearm, from medial epicondyle/radius/ulna to middle phalanges
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the middle phalanges of fingers 2-5

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Heavily used in typing and mouse use - overuse risk

muscles.location: Deep anterior forearm, from ulna and interosseous membrane to distal phalanges
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the distal phalanges of fingers 2-5

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Heavily used in typing - overuse risk

muscles.location: Deep anterior forearm, from radius to distal phalanx of thumb
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the thumb (distal phalanx)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Used in mouse clicking and spacebar pressing

muscles.location: Lateral forearm, from lateral supracondylar ridge to base of 2nd metacarpal
muscles.primary_function: Extends and radially deviates the wrist

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderate from wrist extension on handlebars
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Stabilizes wrist during typing - can develop lateral epicondylitis

muscles.location: Posterior forearm, from lateral epicondyle to base of 3rd metacarpal
muscles.primary_function: Extends and radially deviates the wrist

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Most commonly involved in tennis elbow from repetitive mouse/keyboard use

muscles.location: Posterior forearm, from lateral epicondyle to base of 5th metacarpal
muscles.primary_function: Extends and ulnarly deviates the wrist

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Active during keyboard use

muscles.location: Posterior forearm, from lateral epicondyle to extensor expansions of fingers 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Extends fingers 2-5 at MCP joints

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Active during typing - finger extension between keystrokes

muscles.location: Posterior forearm, from lateral epicondyle to extensor expansion of 5th finger
muscles.primary_function: Extends the little finger

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Some activation during typing

muscles.location: Deep posterior forearm, from ulna to extensor expansion of index finger
muscles.primary_function: Extends the index finger independently

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Used in pointing/mouse clicking motions

muscles.location: Deep posterior forearm, from ulna to distal phalanx of thumb
muscles.primary_function: Extends the distal phalanx of the thumb

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Used in spacebar and keyboard shortcuts

muscles.location: Deep posterior forearm, from radius to proximal phalanx of thumb
muscles.primary_function: Extends the proximal phalanx of the thumb

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Involved in de Quervain's tenosynovitis from repetitive thumb movements

muscles.location: Deep posterior forearm, from ulna/radius to base of 1st metacarpal
muscles.primary_function: Abducts and extends the thumb

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Involved in de Quervain's tenosynovitis from mouse/trackpad use

muscles.location: Upper medial arm, from coracoid process of scapula to mid-humerus
muscles.primary_function: Flexes and adducts the arm at shoulder

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies from disuse

muscles.location: Large fan-shaped muscle covering upper chest, from clavicle, sternum, and ribs to humerus
muscles.primary_function: Flexes, adducts, and internally rotates the arm; assists in breathing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes SHORT and tight from rounded posture, but also loses strength

muscles.location: Deep to pectoralis major, from ribs 3-5 to coracoid process of scapula
muscles.primary_function: Depresses and protracts scapula, assists in breathing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes chronically SHORT and tight - major driver of rounded shoulders

muscles.location: Small muscle between first rib and clavicle
muscles.primary_function: Depresses and stabilizes the clavicle, protects subclavian vessels

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight, contributing to thoracic outlet compression

muscles.location: Lateral chest wall, ribs 1-9 wrapping to scapula
muscles.primary_function: Protracts scapula, holds scapula against chest wall, assists in upward rotation

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens significantly - leads to scapular winging

muscle_groups.region: Posterior trunk - upper, middle, and lower back

muscles.location: Broad muscle covering lower and mid back, from thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, and lower ribs to humerus
muscles.primary_function: Extends, adducts, and internally rotates the arm; pulls body up in climbing

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly - no pulling or overhead movements at desk

muscles.location: Lateral column of erector spinae, from sacrum/iliac crest to ribs and cervical transverse processes
muscles.primary_function: Extends and laterally flexes the spine, maintains upright posture

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Lumbar portion becomes overworked; thoracic portion weakens

muscles.location: Middle column of erector spinae, from sacrum to mastoid process (longest muscle of back)
muscles.primary_function: Extends spine, laterally flexes spine, assists head extension

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Lumbar portion becomes fatigued from sustained sitting; thoracic portion weakens

muscles.location: Medial column of erector spinae, from spinous processes of lumbar/lower thoracic to upper thoracic/cervical
muscles.primary_function: Extends the vertebral column

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes fatigued from sustained postural demands

muscles.location: Deep spinal muscle, from sacrum to C2, spanning 2-4 vertebral segments
muscles.primary_function: Stabilizes individual vertebral segments, fine control of spinal movement

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies RAPIDLY from sitting - key factor in lower back pain. Fat infiltration occurs quickly.

muscles.location: Deepest back muscles, from transverse process of one vertebra to spinous process of vertebra above
muscles.primary_function: Rotates vertebral column, proprioceptive feedback for spinal position

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mildly activated during trunk rotation in gait
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Minimally activated - limited rotation in cycling
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from lack of rotational movement

muscles.location: Deep lower back, from iliac crest to 12th rib and lumbar transverse processes
muscles.primary_function: Lateral flexion of trunk, stabilizes lumbar spine, assists breathing (fixes 12th rib)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for pelvic stabilization during gait
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight on one side from asymmetric sitting - common trigger point source

muscles.location: Deep back, from thoracic transverse processes to spinous processes 4-6 segments above
muscles.primary_function: Extends and rotates the thoracic spine

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from prolonged thoracic flexion

muscles.location: Small muscles between adjacent spinous processes throughout the spine
muscles.primary_function: Assists spinal extension, proprioception of spinal position

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Gradual weakening from sustained flexed posture

muscles.location: Small muscles between adjacent transverse processes throughout the spine
muscles.primary_function: Lateral flexion of spine, spinal stabilization and proprioception

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Gradual weakening

muscles.location: Upper back, from spinous processes C7-T3 to ribs 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Elevates ribs 2-5 during inspiration

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from shallow desk breathing patterns

muscles.location: Lower back, from spinous processes T11-L2 to ribs 9-12
muscles.primary_function: Depresses ribs 9-12 during forced expiration, anchors diaphragm

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from shallow breathing

muscles.location: Deep back/neck, from upper thoracic transverse processes to cervical spinous processes
muscles.primary_function: Extends and rotates cervical spine

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight or weakened depending on posture

muscles.location: Complex connective tissue system of the lower back integrating multiple muscle attachments
muscles.primary_function: Transfers force between upper and lower body, trunk stability

activity_impact.desk_job_only: System becomes dysfunctional - loses tension and load-transfer capacity

muscles.location: Front of abdomen, from pubic symphysis to xiphoid process and costal cartilages 5-7
muscles.primary_function: Flexes trunk (sit-up motion), compresses abdomen, assists breathing

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Mildly activated for trunk flexion position
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens moderately - shortened in seated position but not loaded

muscles.location: Lateral abdomen (superficial), from lower 8 ribs to iliac crest and linea alba
muscles.primary_function: Rotates and laterally flexes trunk, compresses abdomen

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mildly activated from trunk rotation during gait
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse - limited rotational demands at desk

muscles.location: Lateral abdomen (deep to external oblique), from thoracolumbar fascia and iliac crest to lower ribs and linea alba
muscles.primary_function: Rotates and laterally flexes trunk, compresses abdomen, supports core stability

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for trunk rotation and stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Deepest abdominal muscle, wrapping horizontally from thoracolumbar fascia to linea alba
muscles.primary_function: Compresses abdomen, stabilizes spine and pelvis (natural weight belt)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for trunk and pelvic stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly - major contributor to lower back instability

muscles.location: Small triangular muscle in lower abdomen, from pubis to linea alba
muscles.primary_function: Tenses the linea alba (minimal functional role)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal impact - absent in about 20% of population

muscle_groups.region: Hip joint, pelvis, and deep external rotators

muscles.location: Deep core/hip, from lumbar vertebral bodies T12-L5 to lesser trochanter of femur
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the hip, stabilizes the lumbar spine, postural muscle

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during hip flexion phase of gait
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes chronically SHORTENED and tight from prolonged sitting - key pain generator

muscles.location: Lines the iliac fossa of pelvis, joins psoas to insert on lesser trochanter
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the hip (together with psoas forms the iliopsoas)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes shortened and tight from sitting

muscles.location: Largest muscle in the body, from ilium, sacrum, and coccyx to IT band and gluteal tuberosity of femur
muscles.primary_function: Extends and externally rotates the hip; primary muscle for climbing, running, standing from sitting

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately to highly activated during push-off phase
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies RAPIDLY - 'gluteal amnesia' from prolonged compression and disuse

muscles.location: Lateral hip, from outer surface of ilium to greater trochanter of femur
muscles.primary_function: Abducts the hip, stabilizes pelvis during single-leg stance (walking)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated for pelvic stability with every step
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Mildly activated - limited abduction in cycling
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens significantly - causes Trendelenburg gait pattern and knee valgus

muscles.location: Deep to gluteus medius, from outer ilium to greater trochanter
muscles.primary_function: Abducts and internally rotates the hip, assists pelvic stabilization

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Lateral hip, from anterior iliac crest to IT band
muscles.primary_function: Flexes, abducts, and internally rotates the hip; tenses IT band

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes TIGHT and overactive compensating for weak glutes - IT band syndrome risk

muscles.location: Deep buttock, from anterior sacrum through greater sciatic notch to greater trochanter
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates the hip, stabilizes hip joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes tight from prolonged sitting - can compress sciatic nerve (piriformis syndrome)

muscles.location: Deep pelvis, from inner obturator membrane through lesser sciatic notch to greater trochanter
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates the hip, stabilizes hip joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Compressed during sitting - can become tight and irritated

muscles.location: Deep hip, from outer obturator membrane to trochanteric fossa of femur
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates and stabilizes the hip

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Mild weakening from disuse

muscles.location: Deep buttock, from ischial spine to greater trochanter (along obturator internus tendon)
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates the hip

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Compressed during sitting, weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Deep buttock, from ischial tuberosity to greater trochanter
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates the hip

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Compressed during sitting

muscles.location: Deep buttock, flat muscle from ischial tuberosity to intertrochanteric crest of femur
muscles.primary_function: Externally rotates and adducts the hip

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Compressed and weakened from sitting

muscle_groups.region: Anterior, posterior, medial, and lateral thigh

muscles.location: Anterior thigh (superficial), from anterior inferior iliac spine to patella/tibial tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Extends knee and flexes hip (only quad that crosses both joints)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during swing phase and stair climbing
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Shortened from sitting (hip flexion), weakens from lack of knee extension loading

muscles.location: Lateral anterior thigh, from greater trochanter and linea aspera to patella/tibial tuberosity
muscles.primary_function: Extends the knee (largest quadriceps component)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies rapidly - one of the first muscles to show visible loss

muscles.location: Medial anterior thigh, from linea aspera to patella/tibial tuberosity. VMO portion is tear-drop shaped near knee
muscles.primary_function: Extends the knee, VMO stabilizes patella tracking

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated, especially in terminal knee extension
activity_impact.desk_job_only: VMO atrophies fastest of all quad components - leads to patella tracking problems

muscles.location: Deep anterior thigh (beneath rectus femoris), from anterior femoral shaft to patella
muscles.primary_function: Extends the knee

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies with other quads

muscles.location: Longest muscle in body, diagonal from anterior superior iliac spine to medial proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
muscles.primary_function: Flexes, abducts, and externally rotates hip; flexes knee (cross-legged sitting position)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Shortened from sitting posture

muscles.location: Posterior lateral thigh, long head from ischial tuberosity, short head from linea aspera, to fibular head
muscles.primary_function: Flexes knee, extends hip (long head), externally rotates knee

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during late swing phase (decelerating leg)
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and shortens from sitting - common hamstring strain risk factor

muscles.location: Posterior medial thigh, from ischial tuberosity to medial proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
muscles.primary_function: Flexes knee, extends hip, internally rotates knee

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and shortens from prolonged sitting

muscles.location: Posterior medial thigh (deep to semitendinosus), from ischial tuberosity to medial tibial condyle
muscles.primary_function: Flexes knee, extends hip, internally rotates knee

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and shortens

muscles.location: Deep medial thigh, from ischial tuberosity and pubic ramus to linea aspera and adductor tubercle
muscles.primary_function: Adducts thigh, assists hip extension (posterior portion) and hip flexion (anterior portion)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and sustained shortened position

muscles.location: Medial thigh, from pubic body to middle third of linea aspera
muscles.primary_function: Adducts and flexes the thigh

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Shortened from sitting - groin strain risk when suddenly active

muscles.location: Deep medial thigh, from pubis to upper linea aspera
muscles.primary_function: Adducts and flexes the thigh

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Shortened from sitting

muscles.location: Superficial medial thigh, from pubic symphysis to medial proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
muscles.primary_function: Adducts thigh, flexes and internally rotates knee

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens moderately from disuse

muscles.location: Upper medial thigh, from pectineal line of pubis to pectineal line of femur
muscles.primary_function: Adducts, flexes, and internally rotates the thigh

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Shortened from sitting position

muscle_groups.region: Leg between knee and ankle (anterior, lateral, and posterior compartments)

muscles.location: Superficial posterior calf, two heads from medial and lateral femoral condyles to calcaneus via Achilles tendon
muscles.primary_function: Plantarflexes ankle (pushes foot down), assists knee flexion

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated during push-off phase of each step
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies moderately - some maintenance from standing/walking to bathroom

muscles.location: Deep posterior calf (under gastrocnemius), from fibula and tibia to calcaneus via Achilles tendon
muscles.primary_function: Plantarflexes ankle, critical postural muscle preventing forward falling

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated - tonic postural muscle active throughout stance
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse - important venous pump for leg circulation (DVT risk)

muscles.location: Small muscle with long thin tendon, from lateral femoral condyle to calcaneus
muscles.primary_function: Weak ankle plantarflexor and knee flexor (vestigial muscle)

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal functional impact - absent in about 7-10% of population

muscles.location: Anterior shin, from lateral tibia and interosseous membrane to medial cuneiform and 1st metatarsal
muscles.primary_function: Dorsiflexes ankle (lifts foot up), inverts foot, prevents foot slap during walking

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated during swing phase (lifting foot) and heel strike
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Moderately activated maintaining foot position on pedal
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse - shin splint risk when suddenly increasing activity

muscles.location: Deep posterior leg, from tibia, fibula, and interosseous membrane to navicular and multiple tarsal/metatarsal bones
muscles.primary_function: Plantarflexes and inverts foot, primary dynamic support of medial arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated for arch support during stance phase
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens - can lead to flat feet and posterior tibial tendon dysfunction

muscles.location: Lateral leg, from fibular head to base of 1st metatarsal and medial cuneiform (wraps under foot)
muscles.primary_function: Everts foot, plantarflexes ankle, supports transverse arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for lateral ankle stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens - increases ankle sprain risk when resuming activity

muscles.location: Lateral leg, from distal two-thirds of fibula to base of 5th metatarsal
muscles.primary_function: Everts foot, weak ankle plantarflexor

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens - ankle instability risk

muscles.location: Anterior lateral leg, from distal fibula to base of 5th metatarsal
muscles.primary_function: Dorsiflexes and everts the foot

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse - variable presence (absent in some people)

muscles.location: Anterior leg, from lateral tibial condyle and fibula to dorsal expansions of toes 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Extends toes 2-5, dorsiflexes ankle

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Anterior leg (deep), from fibula to distal phalanx of big toe
muscles.primary_function: Extends the big toe, assists dorsiflexion

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated lifting big toe during swing
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Deep posterior leg, from posterior tibia to distal phalanges of toes 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Flexes toes 2-5, plantarflexes ankle, supports longitudinal arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during toe-off phase for grip
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and shoe wearing

muscles.location: Deep posterior leg, from posterior fibula to distal phalanx of big toe
muscles.primary_function: Flexes big toe (critical for push-off in walking/running), plantarflexes ankle

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated during push-off - big toe bears major load
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and restrictive footwear

muscles.location: Posterior knee, from lateral femoral condyle to posterior proximal tibia
muscles.primary_function: Unlocks the knee from full extension, internally rotates tibia, stabilizes knee

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated at initiation of knee flexion each step
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Maintains some tone from standing/sitting transitions

muscle_groups.region: Intrinsic muscles of the foot (dorsal and plantar)

muscles.location: Dorsum of foot, from calcaneus to tendons of toes 2-4
muscles.primary_function: Extends toes 2-4 at MTP joints

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during toe extension in gait
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and restrictive shoes

muscles.location: Dorsum of foot, from calcaneus to proximal phalanx of big toe
muscles.primary_function: Extends the big toe at MTP joint

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Medial plantar foot, from calcaneus to proximal phalanx of big toe
muscles.primary_function: Abducts and flexes big toe, supports medial longitudinal arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated for arch support and big toe stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophies significantly from shoe wearing and disuse - hallux valgus (bunion) risk

muscles.location: Central plantar foot, from calcaneus to middle phalanges of toes 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Flexes toes 2-5 at PIP joints, supports longitudinal arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Highly activated for foot grip and arch support
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and supportive footwear

muscles.location: Lateral plantar foot, from calcaneus to proximal phalanx of 5th toe
muscles.primary_function: Abducts and flexes the little toe, supports lateral arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for lateral foot stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens and atrophies from narrow shoes

muscles.location: Deep plantar foot, from calcaneus to tendon of flexor digitorum longus
muscles.primary_function: Corrects pull direction of flexor digitorum longus, assists toe flexion

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated during toe grip phase
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Plantar foot, from tendons of flexor digitorum longus to extensor expansions of toes 2-5
muscles.primary_function: Flex MTP joints while extending IP joints of toes 2-5 (toe dexterity)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for toe control during push-off
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophy contributes to claw toes and hammer toes

muscles.location: Medial plantar foot, from cuboid and cuneiforms to proximal phalanx of big toe (contains sesamoid bones)
muscles.primary_function: Flexes big toe at MTP joint, critical for push-off in gait

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse and stiff-soled shoes

muscles.location: Deep plantar foot, oblique head from tarsal bones and transverse head from MTP ligaments to proximal phalanx of big toe
muscles.primary_function: Adducts big toe, supports transverse arch

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for transverse arch support
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become tight from narrow shoes contributing to bunions

muscles.romanian_name: Flexorul scurt al degetului mic al piciorului
muscles.latin_name: Musculus flexor digiti minimi brevis pedis
muscles.location: Lateral plantar foot, from base of 5th metatarsal to proximal phalanx of 5th toe
muscles.primary_function: Flexes the little toe

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse

muscles.location: Between metatarsals on plantar side, medial to toes 3-5
muscles.primary_function: Adduct toes 3-5 toward 2nd toe, flex MTP joints

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for toe alignment during stance
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophy contributes to toe deformities

muscles.location: Between metatarsals on dorsal side
muscles.primary_function: Abduct toes 2-4 away from 2nd toe, flex MTP joints

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for toe spread and stability
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Atrophy from narrow shoes and disuse

muscles.location: Lateral plantar foot, from long plantar ligament to 5th metatarsal
muscles.primary_function: Lateral rotation of 5th metatarsal, deepens foot arch

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from supportive footwear disuse

muscle_groups.region: Deep trunk muscles forming the inner stabilization unit

muscles.location: Deepest abdominal layer, wrapping horizontally around trunk like a corset
muscles.primary_function: Primary spinal stabilizer, increases intra-abdominal pressure for core stiffness

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated - anticipatory firing before each step
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Mildly activated for trunk stability on bike
activity_impact.desk_job_only: CRITICAL LOSS - firing timing becomes delayed, major factor in lower back pain

muscles.location: Deep paraspinal muscle, individual segments from sacrum to C2
muscles.primary_function: Segmental spinal stabilization, controls vertebral shear forces

activity_impact.desk_job_only: RAPID atrophy with fat infiltration - does NOT recover spontaneously even when pain resolves

muscles.location: Dome-shaped muscle at base of ribcage separating thorax from abdomen
muscles.primary_function: Provides top-down pressure for core stability (in addition to breathing)

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated - breathing coordination with core stability
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Highly activated from increased breathing demand
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Stabilization function diminishes - chest breathing dominates over diaphragmatic

muscles.location: Sling of muscles at base of pelvis
muscles.primary_function: Provides bottom-up support for core stability, counteracts intra-abdominal pressure

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated - rhythmic engagement with gait
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Variable - saddle pressure can inhibit or compress
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from prolonged sitting and poor breathing - incontinence risk increases

muscles.location: Deep fibers of internal oblique that attach to thoracolumbar fascia
muscles.primary_function: Tensions thoracolumbar fascia for spinal stability, works with transversus abdominis

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for trunk rotation control
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from disuse - core stability compromised

muscles.location: Deep core, attaching to all lumbar vertebral bodies and discs
muscles.primary_function: Stabilizes lumbar spine under compressive loads, maintains lumbar lordosis

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated for lumbar stability during gait
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Actively engaged but in shortened position
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Becomes shortened and dysfunctional - paradoxically both tight AND weak

muscle_groups.region: Thoracic cavity muscles involved in breathing

muscles.location: Large dome-shaped muscle at base of ribcage, central tendon with peripheral muscular fibers from xiphoid, lower ribs, and lumbar vertebrae
muscles.primary_function: Primary muscle of respiration - contracts and descends to create negative pressure for inhalation

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated from increased breathing demand
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Function diminishes - shallow chest breathing pattern develops, reduced excursion range

muscles.location: Between ribs (11 pairs), fibers run obliquely downward and forward
muscles.primary_function: Elevate ribs during inspiration, expanding the thoracic cavity

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Gradual weakening - restricted rib mobility from slouched posture

muscles.location: Between ribs (11 pairs), deep to external intercostals, fibers run obliquely upward and forward
muscles.primary_function: Depress ribs during forced expiration

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Gradual weakening from shallow breathing patterns

muscles.location: Deepest intercostal layer, incomplete - present mainly in lateral chest wall
muscles.primary_function: Assists internal intercostals in forced expiration

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal individual impact

muscles.location: Internal surface of lower ribs, spanning 1-2 rib interspaces
muscles.primary_function: Depress lower ribs during forced expiration

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal individual impact

muscles.location: Inner surface of anterior chest wall, from sternum to costal cartilages 2-6
muscles.primary_function: Depresses costal cartilages during forced expiration

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Minimal individual impact

muscles.location: 12 pairs from transverse processes of C7-T11 to rib below
muscles.primary_function: Elevate ribs during inspiration, assist thoracic lateral flexion and rotation

activity_impact.walking_30min: Mildly activated during breathing and trunk rotation
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Can become restricted from thoracic immobility

muscles.location: From pubic bone to coccyx, forming main part of pelvic floor sling
muscles.primary_function: Supports pelvic organs, controls urination and defecation, core stabilization

activity_impact.walking_30min: Moderately activated with each step - rhythmic pelvic floor engagement
activity_impact.cycling_30min: Variable - saddle can compress or inhibit activation
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from prolonged sitting and poor posture - incontinence risk

muscles.location: From tendinous arch of levator ani to coccyx, forming lateral pelvic floor
muscles.primary_function: Supports pelvic floor, assists in maintaining organ position

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens from sustained pressure and poor posture

muscles.location: From pubic bone forming a U-shaped sling around the rectum
muscles.primary_function: Maintains anorectal angle for fecal continence

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Generally maintained but prolonged sitting alters pelvic floor dynamics

muscles.location: From ischial spine to coccyx and lower sacrum, posterior pelvic floor
muscles.primary_function: Supports pelvic floor posteriorly, flexes coccyx

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Compressed during sitting on hard surfaces

muscles.location: Surrounds the anal canal, attaches to coccyx and perineal body
muscles.primary_function: Voluntary control of defecation, maintains anal continence

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Generally maintained through constant tonic activity

muscles.location: Surrounds the urethra within the pelvic floor
muscles.primary_function: Voluntary control of urination

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Generally maintained but can weaken with overall pelvic floor dysfunction

muscles.location: Perineum, surrounding bulb of penis (males) or vestibule (females)
muscles.primary_function: Assists urination (expelling last drops), sexual function, supports pelvic floor

activity_impact.cycling_30min: Can be compressed by saddle - potential dysfunction
activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens gradually from overall pelvic floor disuse

muscles.location: Perineum, from ischial tuberosity to crus of penis/clitoris
muscles.primary_function: Maintains erection pressure, stabilizes perineum

activity_impact.desk_job_only: Weakens gradually - contributes to pelvic floor dysfunction
